intro: Polyxena angustifolia, also known as Narrow-leaved Polyxena, is a plant species in the family Campanulaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Polyxena angustifolia include flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Polyxena angustifolia is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to support skin health and wound healing.
